title: Word Ladder
problem_statement: A transformation sequence from word beginWord to word endWord using a dictionary wordList is a sequence of words beginWord -> s1 -> s2 -> ... -> sk such that: Every adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter. Every si for 1 <= i <= k is in wordList. All words in the sequence are of the same length. Note that beginWord does not need to be in wordList. sk == endWord. Given two words, beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary wordList, return the number of words in the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord, or 0 if no such sequence exists.

examples.explanation: One shortest transformation sequence is "hit" -> "hot" -> "dot" -> "dog" -> "cog", which is 5 words long.
The endWord "cog" is not in wordList, therefore there is no valid transformation sequence.

constraints: beginWord, endWord, and wordList[i] consist of lowercase English letters.
All the words in wordList are unique.
intuition: Model as graph where words are nodes, edges if differ by one letter. BFS for shortest path.

approaches.name: BFS
approaches.description: Use queue for BFS, generate neighbors by changing one letter. Remove from set to avoid revisit.
interview_commentary: Generate neighbors by changing each letter.
Remove from set to prevent cycles.

approaches.name: Bidirectional BFS
approaches.description: BFS from both start and end, meet in middle.
approaches.why_not: Slower in worst case, but faster average.
interview_commentary: Two sets: from start and end.
Meet when neighbor in other set.

interview_qa.a: Still works, but remove if needed.
